summary: Sunday night was a full automation cycle — all 7 daemons fired, consolidation ran clean across 6 projects, and Dex completed its weekly deep research synthesis. The big win from yesterday's session was Aether migration completion with GPT integration wired in. The persistent sore spots: no newsletter production (Aurora still sandbox-blocked), API health endpoint failing for the 13th consecutive sentinel run, and 2 corrupt JSONL files that Dex keeps flagging. Cipher ran 6 times overnight with zero leaks detected.

accomplished: Completed full system sweep — sentinel, cipher, stale file, broken link, test coverage, and file audit phases all ran
Cipher scans ran 6 times overnight — zero verified leaks across all runs
Sentinel QA run #13: 5 passed, 4 failed — resolved 2 issues from previous run
Consolidation ran at 02:00 MT across all 6 project scopes — completed in 2 seconds
issues: P0 ESCALATION: api-health-green failing 13 consecutive sentinel runs — health endpoint unreachable or founder token unconfigured on Vercel
P0: aurora-ran-today — no newsletter for 2026-04-13 (expected, Aurora blocked on Mini migration)
P1: 15 broken documentation links in website/docs/research/lab/ — all point to newsletters/2026-04-11.md
P1: 5 potentially sensitive files detected outside .secrets/ (including .env.local and a CSV with 'api_tokens' in the name)
Nel improvement score dropped from 85 to 65 — driven by broken links and sentinel findings
agentReports.learned: Score regression 85→65 logged to self-evolution. The broken link pattern is systemic — research lab files reference newsletter paths that don't resolve from the website docs directory. Needs a path fix, not individual link repair.
nextSteps: Fix broken link pattern (path prefix issue, not individual links)
Investigate sensitive file flags — determine if .env.local and CSV are safe to remain
Push for api-health-green resolution — 13 failures is unacceptable
